  1381→async def _close_enterprise_popup(page: Page) -> None:
  1382→    """Close popup/modal that appears after sending a message in enterprise flow."""
  1383→    config = get_config()
  1384→
  1385→    # Try close popup image first
  1386→    close_img_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.close_popup_img")
  1387→    try:
  1388→        close_img = await page.wait_for_selector(close_img_sel, timeout=3000)
  1389→        if close_img:
  1390→            await close_img.click()
  1391→            await page.wait_for_timeout(500)
  1392→    except Exception:
  1393→        pass
  1394→
  1395→    # Try close modal button
  1396→    close_modal_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.close_modal")
  1397→    try:
  1398→        close_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(close_modal_sel, timeout=2000)
  1399→        if close_btn:
  1400→            await close_btn.click()
  1401→            await page.wait_for_timeout(500)
  1402→    except Exception:
  1403→        pass
  1404→
  1405→
  1406→async def send_message_enterprise_inline(
  1407→    page: Page,
  1408→    content: str,
  1409→    config=None,
  1410→) -> bool:
  1411→    """Send a message inline on the enterprise recruitment page.
  1412→
  1413→    This operates on the current visible candidate card — clicks "立即沟通",
  1414→    types the message in the textarea, clicks "发送后留在此页", and closes
  1415→    the popup.
  1416→
  1417→    Args:
  1418→        page: Playwright page on the enterprise results page
  1419→        content: Message content to send
  1420→        config: Config instance (loaded if None)
  1421→
  1422→    Returns:
  1423→        True if message was sent successfully
  1424→    """
  1425→    if config is None:
  1426→        config = get_config()
  1427→
  1428→    try:
  1429→        # Click 立即沟通
  1430→        chat_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.chat_now_button")
  1431→        chat_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(chat_sel, timeout=5000)
  1432→        if not chat_btn:
  1433→            logger.warning("Enterprise: 立即沟通 button not found")
  1434→            return False
  1435→
  1436→        await human_click(page, chat_sel)
  1437→        await async_random_delay("action")
  1438→
  1439→        # Wait for message textarea
  1440→        textarea_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.message_textarea")
  1441→        textarea = await page.wait_for_selector(textarea_sel, timeout=5000)
  1442→        if not textarea:
  1443→            logger.warning("Enterprise: message textarea not found")
  1444→            return False
  1445→
  1446→        # Clear any default text and type message
  1447→        await textarea.click()
  1448→        import sys
  1449→        select_all_key = "Meta+a" if sys.platform == "darwin" else "Control+a"
  1450→        await page.keyboard.press(select_all_key)
  1451→        await page.keyboard.press("Backspace")
  1452→        await human_type(page, textarea_sel, content)
  1453→        await async_random_delay("action")
  1454→
  1455→        # Click 发送后留在此页
  1456→        send_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.send_stay_button")
  1457→        send_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(send_sel, timeout=5000)
  1458→        if not send_btn:
  1459→            logger.warning("Enterprise: send button not found")
  1460→            return False
  1461→
  1462→        await human_click(page, send_sel)
  1463→        await async_random_delay("action")
  1464→
  1465→        # Close popup/modal
  1466→        await _close_enterprise_popup(page)
  1467→
  1468→        # Verify message was actually sent
  1469→        verified = await _verify_message_sent(page, content)
  1470→        if not verified:
  1471→            logger.warning("Enterprise: message send could not be verified")
  1472→            return False
  1473→
  1474→        logger.info("Enterprise: message sent and verified inline")
  1475→        return True
  1476→
  1477→    except Exception as e:
  1478→        logger.error(f"Enterprise inline send failed: {e}")
  1479→        return False
  1480→
  1481→
  1482→async def send_message_batch_enterprise(
  1483→    page: Page,
  1484→    candidates: List[dict],
  1485→    template_name: str,
  1486→    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
  1487→    browser: 'BrowserManager',
  1488→    skip_messaged: bool = True,
  1489→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
  1490→    """Batch send messages inline on the enterprise recruitment page.
  1491→
  1492→    Iterates visible candidate cards on the enterprise results page,
  1493→    sending messages inline without navigating to separate chat pages.
  1494→
  1495→    Args:
  1496→        page: Playwright page on the enterprise results page
  1497→        candidates: List of candidate dicts with maimai_id, name, company, title
  1498→        template_name: Template name to use
  1499→        common_variables: Shared template variables
  1500→        browser: Browser manager instance
  1501→        skip_messaged: Skip already-messaged candidates
  1502→
  1503→    Returns:
  1504→        Dict with total, sent, failed, skipped, rate_limited counts
  1505→    """
  1506→    config = get_config()
  1507→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
  1508→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
  1509→
  1510→    try:
  1511→        template = config.get_template(template_name)
  1512→    except Exception:
  1513→        raise ValidationError(f"Template not found: {template_name}")
  1514→
  1515→    results = {
  1516→        "total": len(candidates),
  1517→        "sent": 0,
  1518→        "failed": 0,
  1519→        "skipped": 0,
  1520→        "rate_limited": 0,
  1521→        "details": [],
  1522→    }
  1523→
  1524→    # Filter already-messaged
  1525→    send_list = []
  1526→    for c in candidates:
  1527→        if skip_messaged:
  1528→            with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
  1529→                existing_msg = session.query(Message).filter_by(
  1530→                    candidate_maimai_id=c["maimai_id"],
  1531→                    status="sent",
  1532→                ).first()
  1533→                if existing_msg:
  1534→                    results["skipped"] += 1
  1535→                    results["details"].append({
  1536→                        "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1537→                        "name": c["name"],
  1538→                        "status": "skipped",
  1539→                        "reason": "already messaged",
  1540→                    })
  1541→                    continue
  1542→        send_list.append(c)
  1543→
  1544→    if not send_list:
  1545→        console.print("[yellow]No candidates to send messages to.[/yellow]")
  1546→        return results
  1547→
  1548→    # Build preview
  1549→    first = send_list[0]
  1550→    first_vars = {
  1551→        **common_variables,
  1552→        "name": first["name"],
  1553→        "company": first["company"],
  1554→        "position": first["title"],
  1555→    }
  1556→    first_content = render_template(template["content"], first_vars)
  1557→
  1558→    # Display summary
  1559→    from rich.table import Table as RichTable
  1560→
  1561→    summary_text = Text()
  1562→    summary_text.append("Template: ", style="bold")
  1563→    summary_text.append(f"{template_name}\n")
  1564→    summary_text.append("Mode: ", style="bold")
  1565→    summary_text.append("Enterprise Inline\n")
  1566→    summary_text.append("Candidates: ", style="bold")
  1567→    summary_text.append(f"{len(candidates)} found, {results['skipped']} already messaged\n")
  1568→    summary_text.append("Will send to: ", style="bold")
  1569→    summary_text.append(f"{len(send_list)} candidates\n\n")
  1570→    summary_text.append("Message Preview ", style="bold cyan")
  1571→    summary_text.append(f"(first candidate):\n", style="dim")
  1572→    summary_text.append("To: ", style="bold cyan")
  1573→    recipient = first["name"]
  1574→    if first.get("title") and first.get("company"):
  1575→        recipient += f" ({first['title']} @ {first['company']})"
  1576→    summary_text.append(f"{recipient}\n\n")
  1577→    summary_text.append(first_content)
  1578→
  1579→    console.print(Panel(
  1580→        summary_text,
  1581→        title="Enterprise Pipeline Summary",
  1582→        border_style="green",
  1583→        padding=(1, 2),
  1584→    ))
  1585→
  1586→    # Confirmation
  1587→    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
  1588→    confirm = await loop.run_in_executor(
  1589→        None,
  1590→        lambda: questionary.confirm(
  1591→            f"Confirm batch send to {len(send_list)} candidates?"
  1592→        ).unsafe_ask(),
  1593→    )
  1594→
  1595→    if not confirm:
  1596→        console.print("[yellow]Batch send cancelled.[/yellow]")
  1597→        return results
  1598→
  1599→    # Send messages
  1600→    from rich.progress import Progress, BarColumn, TextColumn as ProgressTextColumn, MofNCompleteColumn
  1601→
  1602→    with Progress(
  1603→        ProgressTextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
  1604→        BarColumn(),
  1605→        MofNCompleteColumn(),
  1606→        console=console,
  1607→    ) as progress:
  1608→        task = progress.add_task("Sending messages (enterprise)...", total=len(send_list))
  1609→
  1610→        for c in send_list:
  1611→            # Rate limit check
  1612→            try:
  1613→                await async_check_rate_limit("send_message")
  1614→            except RateLimitExceededError as e:
  1615→                console.print(f"\n[yellow]Rate limit reached: {e}[/yellow]")
  1616→                remaining = len(send_list) - (results["sent"] + results["failed"])
  1617→                results["rate_limited"] = remaining
  1618→                results["details"].append({
  1619→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1620→                    "name": c["name"],
  1621→                    "status": "rate_limited",
  1622→                    "reason": str(e),
  1623→                })
  1624→                break
  1625→
  1626→            # Build per-candidate variables
  1627→            variables = {
  1628→                **common_variables,
  1629→                "name": c["name"],
  1630→                "company": c["company"],
  1631→                "position": c["title"],
  1632→            }
  1633→            content = render_template(template["content"], variables)
  1634→
  1635→            # Scroll to find the candidate card (by name match)
  1636→            try:
  1637→                card_locator = page.get_by_text(c["name"], exact=False).first
  1638→                await card_locator.scroll_into_view_if_needed(timeout=5000)
  1639→                await async_random_delay("action")
  1640→            except Exception as e:
  1641→                logger.warning(f"Could not scroll to card for '{c['name']}': {e}")
  1642→
  1643→            # Send inline
  1644→            success = await send_message_enterprise_inline(page, content, config)
  1645→
  1646→            if success:
  1647→                results["sent"] += 1
  1648→                results["details"].append({
  1649→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1650→                    "name": c["name"],
  1651→                    "status": "sent",
  1652→                })
  1653→                rate_limiter.log_action("send_message", {
  1654→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1655→                    "template": template_name,
  1656→                    "mode": "enterprise",
  1657→                })
  1658→                _log_message(db_manager, c["maimai_id"], template_name, content, "sent")
  1659→            else:
  1660→                results["failed"] += 1
  1661→                results["details"].append({
  1662→                    "maimai_id": c["maimai_id"],
  1663→                    "name": c["name"],
  1664→                    "status": "failed",
  1665→                    "reason": "inline send returned False",
  1666→                })
  1667→                _log_message(db_manager, c["maimai_id"], template_name, content, "failed")
  1668→
  1669→            progress.advance(task)
  1670→            await async_random_delay("message")
  1671→
  1672→    return results
  1673→

1→"""Pipeline service for search-and-message orchestration.

     3→Extracted from CLI layer to separate business logic from argument parsing.
     4→Supports dual-path: search_center (Path A) and enterprise (Path B).
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from datetime import datetime

    11→
    12→from rich.console import Console

    15→from src.core.browser import BrowserManager

    17→

    22→    """Search mode for pipeline dispatch."""
    23→    SEARCH_CENTER = "search_center"
    24→    ENTERPRISE = "enterprise"

    27→async def search_all_keywords(
    28→    browser: BrowserManager,
    29→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
    30→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
    31→    location: Optional[str],
    32→    max_pages: int,
    33→    start_page: int = 1,
    34→) -> Tuple[List[CandidateBasic], Set[str]]:
    35→    """Iterate keywords, deduplicate, return combined results.
    36→
    37→    Args:
    38→        browser: BrowserManager instance
    39→        keywords: Keyword strings to search
    40→        company: Company filter strings
    41→        location: Location filter or None
    42→        max_pages: Pages per keyword
    43→        start_page: First page number
    44→
    45→    Returns:
    46→        (deduplicated candidates list, set of seen IDs)
    47→    """
    48→    all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
    49→    seen_ids: Set[str] = set()
    50→
    51→    for idx, kw in enumerate(keywords):
    52→        console.print(f"\n[bold blue][{idx+1}/{len(keywords)}] Searching: {kw}[/bold blue]")
    53→
    54→        candidates = await search_candidates(
    55→            keywords=kw,
    56→            company=company,
    57→            location=location,
    58→            pages=max_pages,
    59→            start_page=start_page,
    60→            browser=browser,
    61→        )
    62→
    63→        new_count = 0
    64→        for c in candidates:
    65→            if c.maimai_id not in seen_ids:
    66→                seen_ids.add(c.maimai_id)
    67→                all_candidates.append(c)
    68→                new_count += 1
    69→
    70→        console.print(f"  Found {len(candidates)} candidates, {new_count} new (deduplicated)")
    71→
    72→    return all_candidates, seen_ids
    73→

   121→async def send_batch(
   122→    candidates: List[CandidateBasic],
   123→    template: str,
   124→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   125→    search_keywords: List[str],
   126→    browser: BrowserManager,
   127→    skip_messaged: bool,
   128→    interactive: bool = False,
   129→) -> dict:
   130→    """Send messages to a batch of candidates, sorted by score.
   131→
   132→    Args:
   133→        candidates: List of CandidateBasic objects
   134→        template: Template name
   135→        variables: Template variables
   136→        search_keywords: Original search keywords (for scoring)
   137→        browser: BrowserManager instance
   138→        skip_messaged: Whether to skip already-messaged candidates
   139→        interactive: Enable interactive selector discovery
   140→
   141→    Returns:
   142→        Results dict with total/sent/failed/skipped/rate_limited counts
   143→    """
   144→    from src.utils.scoring import sort_candidates_by_score
   145→    from src.modules.message import send_message_batch
   146→
   147→    sorted_candidates = sort_candidates_by_score(candidates, search_keywords=search_keywords)
   148→
   149→    candidate_dicts = [
   150→        {
   151→            'maimai_id': c.maimai_id,
   152→            'name': c.name,
   153→            'company': c.company,
   154→            'title': c.title,
   155→            'profile_url': c.profile_url,
   156→        }
   157→        for c in sorted_candidates
   158→    ]
   159→
   160→    return await send_message_batch(
   161→        candidates=candidate_dicts,

   163→        common_variables=variables,
   164→        browser=browser,
   165→        skip_messaged=skip_messaged,
   166→        interactive=interactive,
   167→    )

   217→def generate_pipeline_report(
   218→    candidates: List[CandidateBasic],
   219→    results: dict,
   220→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   221→    template: str,
   222→    mode: SearchMode,
   223→) -> str:
   224→    """Generate an Excel report for a pipeline run.
   225→
   226→    Args:
   227→        candidates: All candidates from search
   228→        results: Send results dict (total/sent/failed/skipped/rate_limited/details)
   229→        keywords: Search keywords used
   230→        template: Template name used
   231→        mode: Search mode (search_center or enterprise)
   232→
   233→    Returns:
   234→        Path to the generated report file
   235→    """
   236→    from openpyxl import Workbook
   237→    from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment, Border, Side
   238→    from loguru import logger
   239→
   240→    timestamp = datetime.now()
   241→    report_dir = Path("data/reports")
   242→    report_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   243→    report_path = report_dir / f"pipeline_{timestamp.strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}.xlsx"
   244→
   245→    wb = Workbook()
   246→
   247→    # --- Sheet 1: Overview ---
   248→    ws_overview = wb.active
   249→    ws_overview.title = "概览"
   250→
   251→    header_font = Font(bold=True, color="FFFFFF")
   252→    header_fill = PatternFill(start_color="4472C4", end_color="4472C4", fill_type="solid")
   253→    border = Border(
   254→        left=Side(style="thin"), right=Side(style="thin"),
   255→        top=Side(style="thin"), bottom=Side(style="thin"),

   257→
   258→    overview_rows = [

   264→        ("发送总数", results["sent"] + results["failed"]),
   265→        ("成功", results["sent"]),
   266→        ("失败", results["failed"]),
   267→        ("跳过(已沟通)", results["skipped"]),
   268→        ("限流", results["rate_limited"]),
   269→    ]
   270→
   271→    for r_idx, (key, value) in enumerate(overview_rows, 1):
   272→        cell_key = ws_overview.cell(row=r_idx, column=1, value=key)
   273→        cell_val = ws_overview.cell(row=r_idx, column=2, value=value)
   274→        cell_key.font = Font(bold=True)
   275→        for cell in (cell_key, cell_val):
   276→            cell.border = border
   277→            cell.alignment = Alignment(vertical="center")
   278→
   279→    ws_overview.column_dimensions["A"].width = 18
   280→    ws_overview.column_dimensions["B"].width = 40
   281→
   282→    # --- Sheet 2: Candidate Details ---
   283→    ws_details = wb.create_sheet(title="候选人详情")
   284→
   285→    columns = ["序号", "姓名", "职位", "公司", "地区", "脉脉ID", "沟通状态", "失败原因"]
   286→    for c_idx, col_name in enumerate(columns, 1):
   287→        cell = ws_details.cell(row=1, column=c_idx, value=col_name)
   288→        cell.font = header_font
   289→        cell.fill = header_fill
   290→        cell.alignment = Alignment(horizontal="center", vertical="center")
   291→        cell.border = border
   292→
   293→    # Build a lookup from maimai_id -> detail
   294→    details_by_id: Dict[str, dict] = {}
   295→    for detail in results.get("details", []):
   296→        details_by_id[detail["maimai_id"]] = detail
   297→
   298→    status_map = {
   299→        "sent": "已发送",
   300→        "failed": "失败",
   301→        "skipped": "跳过",
   302→        "rate_limited": "限流",
   303→    }
   304→
   305→    for r_idx, c in enumerate(candidates, 1):
   306→        detail = details_by_id.get(c.maimai_id, {})
   307→        raw_status = detail.get("status", "not_attempted")
   308→        status_label = status_map.get(raw_status, "未处理")
   309→        reason = detail.get("reason", "")
   310→
   311→        row_data = [r_idx, c.name, c.title, c.company, c.location or "", c.maimai_id, status_label, reason]
   312→        for c_idx, value in enumerate(row_data, 1):
   313→            cell = ws_details.cell(row=r_idx + 1, column=c_idx, value=value)
   314→            cell.border = border
   315→            cell.alignment = Alignment(vertical="center", wrap_text=True)
   316→
   317→    detail_widths = {"A": 6, "B": 12, "C": 25, "D": 20, "E": 12, "F": 18, "G": 12, "H": 30}
   318→    for col, width in detail_widths.items():
   319→        ws_details.column_dimensions[col].width = width
   320→
   321→    ws_details.freeze_panes = "A2"
   322→    ws_details.auto_filter.ref = ws_details.dimensions
   323→
   324→    wb.save(str(report_path))
   325→    logger.info(f"Pipeline report saved to: {report_path}")
   326→    console.print(f"[green]Pipeline report saved to: {report_path}[/green]")
   327→    return str(report_path)

   330→def display_candidates_table(candidates: List[CandidateBasic], title: str) -> None:
   331→    """Display a Rich table of candidates."""
   332→    table = Table(title=f"{title} ({len(candidates)} candidates)")
   333→    table.add_column("#", style="dim", justify="right")
   334→    table.add_column("Name", style="cyan")
   335→    table.add_column("Title", style="green")
   336→    table.add_column("Company", style="yellow")
   337→    table.add_column("Location", style="blue")
   338→    has_scores = any(hasattr(c, '_score') for c in candidates[:1])
   339→    if has_scores:
   340→        table.add_column("Score", style="magenta", justify="right")
   341→    table.add_column("ID", style="dim")
   342→
   343→    for i, c in enumerate(candidates[:50], 1):
   344→        row = [str(i), c.name, c.title, c.company, c.location or '']
   345→        if has_scores:
   346→            row.append(str(getattr(c, '_score', '')))
   347→        row.append(c.maimai_id)
   348→        table.add_row(*row)
   349→
   350→    console.print(table)
   351→
   352→    if len(candidates) > 50:
   353→        console.print(f"[dim]... and {len(candidates) - 50} more[/dim]")
   354→

   356→def display_send_results(results: dict, title: str = "Send Results") -> None:
   357→    """Display send results summary table."""

   360→    result_table.add_column("Metric", style="bold")

   363→    result_table.add_row("Total candidates", str(results['total']))
   364→    result_table.add_row("Sent", f"[green]{results['sent']}[/green]")
   365→    result_table.add_row("Failed", f"[red]{results['failed']}[/red]")
   366→    result_table.add_row("Skipped (already messaged)", f"[yellow]{results['skipped']}[/yellow]")
   367→    result_table.add_row("Rate limited", f"[yellow]{results['rate_limited']}[/yellow]")
   368→
   369→    console.print(result_table)

   371→
   372→async def run_pipeline_default(
   373→    browser: BrowserManager,
   374→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   375→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
   376→    location: Optional[str],
   377→    max_pages: int,
   378→    template: str,
   379→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   380→    dry_run: bool,
   381→    skip_messaged: bool,
   382→    interactive: bool = False,
   383→    mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.SEARCH_CENTER,
   384→) -> None:
   385→    """Pipeline: search all pages first, then send messages.
   386→
   387→    Saves a checkpoint after search completes so that message sending
   388→    can be resumed if interrupted.
   389→    """
   390→    from src.utils.checkpoint import CheckpointManager
   391→
   392→    checkpoint = CheckpointManager()
   393→
   394→    if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   395→        # Keep enterprise tab open for inline messaging (unless dry_run)
   396→        all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
   397→            browser, keywords, company, max_pages,
   398→            keep_last_tab_open=not dry_run,
   399→        )
   400→    else:
   401→        all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords(
   402→            browser, keywords, company, location, max_pages,
   403→        )
   404→
   405→    if not all_candidates:
   406→        console.print("[yellow]No candidates found for any keyword.[/yellow]")
   407→        return
   408→
   409→    # Save checkpoint after search (batch_pages=0 marks non-batch mode)
   410→    _save_checkpoint(
   411→        checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   412→        max_pages, 0, skip_messaged, interactive,
   413→        max_pages, [], seen_ids, {'total': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0},
   414→        mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   415→    )
   416→
   417→    display_candidates_table(all_candidates, "Pipeline Results")
   418→
   419→    if dry_run:
   420→        console.print(f"\n[cyan][Dry Run] {len(all_candidates)} candidates found. No messages sent.[/cyan]")
   421→        checkpoint.delete()
   422→        return
   423→
   424→    if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   425→        results = await send_batch_enterprise(
   426→            all_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged,
   427→        )
   428→    else:
   429→        results = await send_batch(
   430→            all_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged, interactive,
   431→        )
   432→    display_send_results(results)
   433→    generate_pipeline_report(all_candidates, results, keywords, template, mode)
   434→
   435→    # Clean up checkpoint on successful completion
   436→    checkpoint.delete()
   437→    console.print("[dim]Checkpoint cleared (pipeline complete)[/dim]")
   438→
   439→
   440→async def run_pipeline_batched(
   441→    browser: BrowserManager,
   442→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   443→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
   444→    location: Optional[str],
   445→    max_pages: int,
   446→    batch_pages: int,
   447→    template: str,
   448→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   449→    dry_run: bool,
   450→    skip_messaged: bool,
   451→    interactive: bool = False,
   452→    resume_ckpt=None,
   453→    mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.SEARCH_CENTER,
   454→) -> None:
   455→    """Batched pipeline: search N pages, send messages, repeat."""
   456→    from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in, check_login_status
   457→    from src.core.session import get_session_manager
   458→    from src.utils.checkpoint import CheckpointManager
   459→
   460→    # Restore state from checkpoint or start fresh
   461→    if resume_ckpt:
   462→        # On resume, only treat sent_ids as truly "seen" (skip-worthy).
   463→        # Candidates that were found but not sent should be retried.
   464→        sent_ids = list(resume_ckpt.sent_ids)
   465→        seen_ids = set(sent_ids)  # only skip candidates already successfully sent
   466→        total_results = resume_ckpt.total_results or {
   467→            'total': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0,
   468→        }
   469→        start_from_page = resume_ckpt.completed_pages + 1
   470→        console.print(f"[cyan]Resuming from page {start_from_page} (sent: {len(sent_ids)}, retrying unsent)[/cyan]")
   471→        checkpoint = resume_ckpt
   472→    else:
   473→        seen_ids = set()
   474→        sent_ids = []
   475→        total_results = {'total': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0}
   476→        start_from_page = 1
   477→        checkpoint = CheckpointManager()
   478→
   479→    all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
   480→    all_details: List[dict] = []
   481→    batch_num = 0
   482→    consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   483→
   484→    batch_start = start_from_page
   485→    while batch_start <= max_pages:
   486→        batch_page_count = min(batch_pages, max_pages - batch_start + 1)
   487→        batch_num += 1
   488→        batch_end = batch_start + batch_page_count - 1
   489→
   490→        console.print(f"\n[bold magenta]━━━ Batch {batch_num}: Pages {batch_start}-{batch_end} ━━━[/bold magenta]")
   491→
   492→        if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   493→            batch_candidates, _ = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
   494→                browser, keywords, company, batch_page_count,
   495→                keep_last_tab_open=not dry_run,
   496→            )
   497→        else:
   498→            batch_candidates, _ = await search_all_keywords(
   499→                browser, keywords, company, location, batch_page_count, start_page=batch_start,
   500→            )
   501→        # Filter out globally seen IDs
   502→        batch_candidates = [c for c in batch_candidates if c.maimai_id not in seen_ids]
   503→        seen_ids.update(c.maimai_id for c in batch_candidates)
   504→        all_candidates.extend(batch_candidates)
   505→
   506→        if not batch_candidates:
   507→            consecutive_empty_batches += 1
   508→            console.print(f"[yellow]No new candidates in batch {batch_num}.[/yellow]")
   509→
   510→            if consecutive_empty_batches >= 2:
   511→                console.print("[red]Multiple consecutive empty batches detected. Verifying login status...[/red]")
   512→                if not await check_login_status(browser.page):
   513→                    console.print("[red]Session expired! Clearing session and re-authenticating...[/red]")
   514→                    session_manager = get_session_manager()
   515→                    await session_manager.clear()
   516→                    await ensure_logged_in(browser)
   517→                    consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   518→                    console.print("[green]Re-authenticated. Retrying current batch...[/green]")
   519→                    batch_num -= 1
   520→                    continue
   521→                else:
   522→                    console.print("[yellow]Session appears valid. Possibly no more results for these keywords.[/yellow]")
   523→
   524→            _save_checkpoint(
   525→                checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   526→                max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   527→                batch_end, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   528→                mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   529→            )
   530→            batch_start += batch_pages
   531→            continue
   532→        else:
   533→            consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   534→
   535→        display_candidates_table(batch_candidates, f"Batch {batch_num} Results")
   536→
   537→        if dry_run:
   538→            console.print(f"\n[cyan][Dry Run] Batch {batch_num}: {len(batch_candidates)} candidates found. No messages sent.[/cyan]")
   539→            _save_checkpoint(
   540→                checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   541→                max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   542→                batch_end, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   543→                mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   544→            )
   545→            batch_start += batch_pages
   546→            continue
   547→
   548→        if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   549→            results = await send_batch_enterprise(
   550→                batch_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged,
   551→            )
   552→        else:
   553→            results = await send_batch(
   554→                batch_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged, interactive,
   555→            )
   556→        display_send_results(results, title=f"Batch {batch_num} Send Results")
   557→
   558→        all_details.extend(results.get('details', []))
   559→        for detail in results.get('details', []):
   560→            if detail.get('status') == 'sent':
   561→                sent_ids.append(detail['maimai_id'])
   562→
   563→        for key in total_results:
   564→            total_results[key] += results[key]
   565→
   566→        _save_checkpoint(
   567→            checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   568→            max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   569→            batch_end, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   570→            mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   571→        )
   572→        console.print(f"[dim]Checkpoint saved (page {batch_end})[/dim]")
   573→
   574→        batch_start += batch_pages
   575→
   576→    # Final summary
   577→    console.print(f"\n[bold magenta]━━━ Pipeline Complete ━━━[/bold magenta]")
   578→    console.print(f"Total unique candidates found: {len(seen_ids)}")
   579→    if not dry_run and total_results['total'] > 0:
   580→        display_send_results(total_results, title="Overall Send Results")
   581→        total_results_with_details = {**total_results, 'details': all_details}
   582→        generate_pipeline_report(all_candidates, total_results_with_details, keywords, template, mode)
   583→
   584→    checkpoint.delete()
   585→    console.print("[dim]Checkpoint cleared (pipeline complete)[/dim]")
   586→
   587→
   588→def _save_checkpoint(
   589→    checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   590→    max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   591→    completed_pages, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   592→    mode: str = "search_center",
   593→    dry_run: bool = False,
   594→) -> None:
   595→    """Save pipeline checkpoint (DRY helper)."""
   596→    checkpoint.save(
   597→        keywords=keywords, template=template, variables=variables,
   598→        company=company, location=location, max_pages=max_pages,
   599→        batch_pages=batch_pages, skip_messaged=skip_messaged,
   600→        interactive=interactive, completed_pages=completed_pages,
   601→        sent_ids=sent_ids, seen_ids=list(seen_ids),
   602→        total_results=total_results, mode=mode, dry_run=dry_run,
   603→    )
   604→

1→"""Configuration loader and manager."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→from pathlib import Path
     5→from typing import Any, Dict
     6→
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→
     9→from src.exceptions.errors import ConfigurationError
    10→
    11→
    12→class Config:
    13→    """Configuration manager for Maimai Assistant."""
    14→
    15→    def __init__(self, config_dir: str = "config"):
    16→        self.config_dir = Path(config_dir)
    17→        self._settings: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    18→        self._selectors: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    19→        self._message_templates: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    20→
    21→        self._load_all()
    22→
    23→    def _load_json(self, filename: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    24→        """Load JSON configuration file."""
    25→        filepath = self.config_dir / filename
    26→        try:
    27→            with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    28→                return json.load(f)
    29→        except FileNotFoundError:
    30→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Configuration file not found: {filepath}")
    31→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
    32→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid JSON in {filepath}: {e}")
    33→
    34→    def _load_all(self):
    35→        """Load all configuration files."""
    36→        logger.info("Loading configuration files...")
    37→        self._settings = self._load_json("settings.json")
    38→        self._selectors = self._load_json("selectors.json")
    39→        self._message_templates = self._load_json("message_templates.json")
    40→        logger.info("Configuration loaded successfully")
    41→
    42→    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    43→        """Get setting by dot-notation key (e.g., 'browser.headless')."""
    44→        keys = key.split('.')
    45→        value = self._settings
    46→
    47→        for k in keys:
    48→            if isinstance(value, dict):
    49→                value = value.get(k)
    50→                if value is None:
    51→                    return default
    52→            else:
    53→                return default
    54→
    55→        return value
    56→
    57→    def get_selector(self, selector_path: str) -> str:
    58→        """Get selector by dot-notation path (e.g., 'search.keyword_input')."""
    59→        keys = selector_path.split('.')
    60→        value = self._selectors.get('selectors', {})
    61→
    62→        for k in keys:
    63→            if isinstance(value, dict):
    64→                value = value.get(k)
    65→                if value is None:
    66→                    raise ConfigurationError(f"Selector not found: {selector_path}")
    67→            else:
    68→                raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid selector path: {selector_path}")
    69→
    70→        return value
    71→
    72→    def get_template(self, template_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    73→        """Get message template by name."""
    74→        template = self._message_templates.get(template_name)
    75→        if template is None:
    76→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Message template not found: {template_name}")
    77→        return template
    78→
    79→    def list_templates(self) -> list:
    80→        """List all available message template names."""
    81→        return list(self._message_templates.keys())
    82→
    83→
    84→# Global configuration instance
    85→_config_instance = None
    86→
    87→
    88→def get_config() -> Config:
    89→    """Get global configuration instance."""
    90→    global _config_instance
    91→    if _config_instance is None:
    92→        _config_instance = Config()
    93→    return _config_instance
    94→
    95→
    96→

1→"""Rate limiter for preventing account bans."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→from datetime import datetime, timedelta
     5→from typing import Tuple, Optional
     6→
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→from sqlalchemy import func
     9→
    10→from src.models.database import ActionLog, get_db_manager, DatabaseManager
    11→from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    12→from src.exceptions.errors import RateLimitExceededError
    13→
    14→
    15→class RateLimiter:
    16→    """Rate limiter for controlling action frequency.
    17→
    18→    Supports adaptive throttling: when captchas or errors are reported,
    19→    limits are temporarily tightened to reduce detection risk.
    20→    """
    21→
    22→    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Config] = None, db_manager: Optional[DatabaseManager] = None):
    23→        """Initialize rate limiter with configuration.
    24→
    25→        Args:
    26→            config: Config instance (uses global singleton if None)
    27→            db_manager: DatabaseManager instance (uses global singleton if None)
    28→        """
    29→        self.config = config or get_config()
    30→        self.db_manager = db_manager or get_db_manager()
    31→
    32→        # Adaptive throttle state
    33→        self._throttle_factor: float = 1.0  # 1.0 = normal, <1.0 = stricter
    34→        self._captcha_timestamps: list = []
    35→        self._error_timestamps: list = []
    36→        self._pruned: bool = False  # one-time prune flag
    37→
    38→    def report_captcha(self) -> None:
    39→        """Report a captcha occurrence to trigger adaptive throttling."""
    40→        now = datetime.utcnow()
    41→        self._captcha_timestamps.append(now)
    42→        # Keep only last 30 minutes of captcha events
    43→        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
    44→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    45→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    46→
    47→    def report_error(self) -> None:
    48→        """Report a request error/failure to trigger adaptive throttling."""
    49→        now = datetime.utcnow()
    50→        self._error_timestamps.append(now)
    51→        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
    52→        self._error_timestamps = [t for t in self._error_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    53→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    54→
    55→    def _update_throttle_factor(self) -> None:
    56→        """Recalculate throttle factor based on recent captcha/error frequency.
    57→
    58→        Throttle factor ranges from 1.0 (normal) to 0.3 (heavily throttled).
    59→        - 1 captcha in 30min: reduce to 0.7x (30% fewer operations allowed)
    60→        - 2 captchas: reduce to 0.5x
    61→        - 3+ captchas: reduce to 0.3x
    62→        - Errors contribute at half the weight of captchas
    63→        """
    64→        captcha_count = len(self._captcha_timestamps)
    65→        error_count = len(self._error_timestamps)
    66→        combined = captcha_count + error_count * 0.5
    67→
    68→        if combined >= 3:
    69→            self._throttle_factor = 0.3
    70→        elif combined >= 2:
    71→            self._throttle_factor = 0.5
    72→        elif combined >= 1:
    73→            self._throttle_factor = 0.7
    74→        else:
    75→            self._throttle_factor = 1.0
    76→
    77→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
    78→            logger.warning(
    79→                f"Adaptive throttle active: factor={self._throttle_factor:.1f} "
    80→                f"(captchas={captcha_count}, errors={error_count} in last 30min)"
    81→            )
    82→
    83→    def _refresh_throttle(self) -> None:
    84→        """Prune expired timestamps and recalculate throttle factor."""
    85→        cutoff = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(minutes=30)
    86→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    87→        self._error_timestamps = [t for t in self._error_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    88→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    89→
    90→    @property
    91→    def throttle_factor(self) -> float:
    92→        """Current adaptive throttle factor (1.0 = normal, <1.0 = throttled)."""
    93→        return self._throttle_factor
    94→
    95→    def _get_limits(self, action_type: str) -> Tuple[int, int, int]:
    96→        """
    97→        Get rate limits for an action type, adjusted by adaptive throttle.
    98→
    99→        Args:
   100→            action_type: Type of action ('search', 'view_profile', 'send_message')
   101→
   102→        Returns:
   103→            Tuple of (per_hour_limit, per_day_limit, min_interval_seconds)
   104→        """
   105→        action_map = {
   106→            'search': ('search_per_hour', 'search_per_day'),
   107→            'view_profile': ('profile_view_per_hour', 'profile_view_per_day'),
   108→            'send_message': ('message_per_hour', 'message_per_day'),
   109→        }
   110→
   111→        if action_type not in action_map:
   112→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown action type: {action_type}")
   113→
   114→        hour_key, day_key = action_map[action_type]
   115→
   116→        per_hour = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{hour_key}', 20)
   117→        per_day = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{day_key}', 100)
   118→        min_interval = self.config.get('rate_limits.min_interval_seconds', 30)
   119→
   120→        # Apply adaptive throttle: reduce limits and increase interval
   121→        per_hour = max(1, int(per_hour * self._throttle_factor))
   122→        per_day = max(1, int(per_day * self._throttle_factor))
   123→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
   124→            min_interval = int(min_interval / self._throttle_factor)
   125→
   126→        return per_hour, per_day, min_interval
   127→
   128→    def _count_actions(self, action_type: str, since: datetime) -> int:
   129→        """
   130→        Count actions of a type since a given time.
   131→
   132→        Args:
   133→            action_type: Type of action
   134→            since: Count actions since this time
   135→
   136→        Returns:
   137→            Number of actions
   138→        """
   139→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   140→            count = session.query(func.count(ActionLog.id)).filter(
   141→                ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   142→                ActionLog.timestamp >= since
   143→            ).scalar()
   144→
   145→            return count or 0
   146→
   147→    def _get_last_action_time(self, action_type: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[datetime]:
   148→        """
   149→        Get timestamp of last action.
   150→
   151→        Args:
   152→            action_type: Type of action, or None for any action
   153→
   154→        Returns:
   155→            Timestamp of last action, or None if no actions
   156→        """
   157→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   158→            query = session.query(func.max(ActionLog.timestamp))
   159→
   160→            if action_type:
   161→                query = query.filter(ActionLog.action_type == action_type)
   162→
   163→            last_time = query.scalar()
   164→            return last_time
   165→
   166→    def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
   167→        """
   168→        Check if action is allowed under rate limits.
   169→
   170→        Args:
   171→            action_type: Type of action to check
   172→
   173→        Raises:
   174→            RateLimitExceededError: If rate limit is exceeded
   175→        """
   176→        # Prune stale entries once per session to keep counts accurate
   177→        if not self._pruned:
   178→            self.prune_old_entries()
   179→            self._pruned = True
   180→
   181→        self._refresh_throttle()
   182→        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)
   183→
   184→        now = datetime.utcnow()
   185→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   186→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   187→
   188→        # Check hourly limit
   189→        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   190→        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
   191→            # Calculate wait time until oldest action expires
   192→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   193→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   194→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   195→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
   196→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   197→
   198→                if oldest_in_window:
   199→                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(hours=1)
   200→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   201→                else:
   202→                    wait_seconds = 3600
   203→
   204→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   205→                action_type=action_type,
   206→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   207→                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   208→                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
   209→            )
   210→
   211→        # Check daily limit
   212→        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   213→        if daily_count >= per_day:
   214→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   215→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   216→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   217→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
   218→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   219→
   220→                if oldest_in_window:
   221→                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(days=1)
   222→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   223→                else:
   224→                    wait_seconds = 86400
   225→
   226→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   227→                action_type=action_type,
   228→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   229→                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   230→                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
   231→            )
   232→
   233→        # Check minimum interval
   234→        last_action = self._get_last_action_time()
   235→        if last_action:
   236→            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
   237→            if elapsed < min_interval:
   238→                wait_seconds = max(1, int(min_interval - elapsed))
   239→                raise RateLimitExceededError(
   240→                    action_type='any',
   241→                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
   242→                    message=f"Minimum interval not met. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
   243→                )
   244→
   245→        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")
   246→
   247→    def log_action(self, action_type: str, details: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
   248→        """
   249→        Log an action to the database.
   250→
   251→        Args:
   252→            action_type: Type of action
   253→            details: Optional action details
   254→        """
   255→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   256→            action_log = ActionLog(
   257→                action_type=action_type,
   258→                timestamp=datetime.utcnow(),
   259→                details=details or {}
   260→            )
   261→            session.add(action_log)
   262→
   263→        logger.debug(f"Logged action: {action_type}")
   264→
   265→    def prune_old_entries(self, retention_hours: int = 48) -> int:
   266→        """Remove ActionLog entries older than the retention window.
   267→
   268→        This prevents stale counts from accumulating across sessions and
   269→        keeps the ActionLog table from growing indefinitely.
   270→
   271→        Args:
   272→            retention_hours: Delete entries older than this many hours (default 48)
   273→
   274→        Returns:
   275→            Number of entries deleted
   276→        """
   277→        cutoff = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=retention_hours)
   278→        try:
   279→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   280→                count = session.query(ActionLog).filter(
   281→                    ActionLog.timestamp < cutoff
   282→                ).delete()
   283→                logger.debug(f"Pruned {count} old ActionLog entries (older than {retention_hours}h)")
   284→                return count
   285→        except Exception as e:
   286→            logger.error(f"Failed to prune ActionLog entries: {e}")
   287→            return 0
   288→
   289→    def get_quota_status(self, action_type: str) -> dict:
   290→        """
   291→        Get current quota status for an action type.
   292→
   293→        Args:
   294→            action_type: Type of action
   295→
   296→        Returns:
   297→            Dictionary with quota information
   298→        """
   299→        per_hour, per_day, _ = self._get_limits(action_type)
   300→
   301→        now = datetime.utcnow()
   302→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   303→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   304→
   305→        hourly_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   306→        daily_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   307→
   308→        return {
   309→            'action_type': action_type,
   310→            'hourly': {
   311→                'used': hourly_used,
   312→                'limit': per_hour,
   313→                'remaining': max(0, per_hour - hourly_used)
   314→            },
   315→            'daily': {
   316→                'used': daily_used,
   317→                'limit': per_day,
   318→                'remaining': max(0, per_day - daily_used)
   319→            }
   320→        }
   321→
   322→
   323→async def async_check_rate_limit(action_type: str, max_retries: int = 60) -> None:
   324→    """
   325→    Check rate limit, waiting through any violations until the action is allowed.
   326→
   327→    Blocks until the rate limit clears. Raises RateLimitExceededError if
   328→    still blocked after max_retries attempts (prevents infinite loops from
   329→    persistent failures such as corrupt DB data).
   330→
   331→    Args:
   332→        action_type: Type of action to check
   333→        max_retries: Maximum wait-retry cycles before giving up
   334→    """
   335→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   336→    for attempt in range(max_retries):
   337→        try:
   338→            rate_limiter.check_rate_limit(action_type)
   339→            return
   340→        except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   341→            if attempt >= max_retries - 1:
   342→                raise
   343→            wait = e.wait_time + 1  # +1s buffer
   344→            logger.info(f"Rate limit hit: {e}. Waiting {wait}s before retrying (attempt {attempt + 1}/{max_retries})...")
   345→            await asyncio.sleep(wait)
   346→
   347→
   348→# Global rate limiter instance
   349→_rate_limiter = None
   350→
   351→
   352→def get_rate_limiter() -> RateLimiter:
   353→    """Get global rate limiter instance."""
   354→    global _rate_limiter
   355→    if _rate_limiter is None:
   356→        _rate_limiter = RateLimiter()
   357→    return _rate_limiter
   358→

1→"""Random delay generators for human-like behavior simulation.
     2→
     3→Uses log-normal distribution instead of uniform for more realistic timing.
     4→Includes fatigue simulation (delays increase over session duration) and
     5→random "distraction" pauses to mimic real human behavior.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import math
     9→import random
    10→import asyncio
    11→import time
    12→from loguru import logger
    13→from src.utils.config import get_config
    14→
    15→
    16→# Session start time for fatigue calculation
    17→_session_start: float = time.monotonic()
    18→
    19→# Action counter for distraction triggers
    20→_action_count: int = 0
    21→
    22→
    23→def _reset_session():
    24→    """Reset session tracking. Call at the start of each CLI command."""
    25→    global _session_start, _action_count
    26→    _session_start = time.monotonic()
    27→    _action_count = 0
    28→
    29→
    30→def reset_session():
    31→    """Public API: reset fatigue/distraction tracking for a new command invocation."""
    32→    _reset_session()
    33→
    34→
    35→def _lognormal_delay(min_val: float, max_val: float) -> float:
    36→    """Generate a log-normal distributed delay between min and max.
    37→
    38→    Log-normal produces a right-skewed distribution: most delays are
    39→    near the low end, with occasional longer pauses — matching human behavior.
    40→    """
    41→    # Map min/max to log-normal mu/sigma
    42→    mid = (min_val + max_val) / 2
    43→    mu = math.log(mid)
    44→    sigma = 0.4  # Controls spread; 0.4 gives moderate variance
    45→    delay = random.lognormvariate(mu, sigma)
    46→    return max(min_val, min(delay, max_val * 1.5))
    47→
    48→
    49→def _fatigue_multiplier() -> float:
    50→    """Calculate fatigue multiplier based on session duration.
    51→
    52→    Humans slow down over time. After 30 minutes, delays increase by ~20%.
    53→    After 1 hour, by ~40%. Caps at 1.6x.
    54→    """
    55→    elapsed_minutes = (time.monotonic() - _session_start) / 60.0
    56→    if elapsed_minutes < 10:
    57→        return 1.0
    58→    # Gradual ramp: 1.0 at 10min → ~1.6 at 90min
    59→    factor = 1.0 + 0.006 * min(elapsed_minutes - 10, 100)
    60→    return min(factor, 1.6)
    61→
    62→
    63→def _maybe_distraction_pause() -> float:
    64→    """Occasionally return a long "distraction" pause.
    65→
    66→    About 3% chance of a 5-30 second pause (simulating checking phone,
    67→    reading something, etc.). Returns 0 most of the time.
    68→    """
    69→    global _action_count
    70→    _action_count += 1
    71→
    72→    # Don't trigger on first few actions
    73→    if _action_count < 5:
    74→        return 0.0
    75→
    76→    if random.random() < 0.03:
    77→        pause = random.uniform(5.0, 30.0)
    78→        logger.debug(f"Simulating distraction pause: {pause:.1f}s")
    79→        return pause
    80→    return 0.0
    81→
    82→
    83→def _time_of_day_multiplier() -> float:
    84→    """Adjust delays based on time of day.
    85→
    86→    Late night (23:00-07:00): slower (1.3x) — fewer people browse late.
    87→    Peak hours (10:00-12:00, 14:00-17:00): normal (1.0x).
    88→    Other times: slightly slower (1.1x).
    89→    """
    90→    import datetime
    91→    hour = datetime.datetime.now().hour
    92→
    93→    if 23 <= hour or hour < 7:
    94→        return 1.3
    95→    elif (10 <= hour <= 12) or (14 <= hour <= 17):
    96→        return 1.0
    97→    else:
    98→        return 1.1
    99→
   100→
   101→def get_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action') -> float:
   102→    """
   103→    Get a random delay based on configured ranges.
   104→
   105→    Uses log-normal distribution with fatigue, distraction, and
   106→    time-of-day adjustments for realistic human behavior.
   107→
   108→    Args:
   109→        delay_type: Type of delay - 'page_load', 'action', 'typing', 'scroll', 'message'
   110→
   111→    Returns:
   112→        Random delay in seconds
   113→    """
   114→    config = get_config()
   115→
   116→    delay_ranges = {
   117→        'page_load': (
   118→            config.get('delays.page_load_min', 2),
   119→            config.get('delays.page_load_max', 5)
   120→        ),
   121→        'action': (
   122→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
   123→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
   124→        ),
   125→        'typing': (
   126→            config.get('delays.typing_min', 0.05),
   127→            config.get('delays.typing_max', 0.15)
   128→        ),
   129→        'scroll': (
   130→            config.get('delays.scroll_min', 0.5),
   131→            config.get('delays.scroll_max', 1.5)
   132→        ),
   133→        'message': (
   134→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
   135→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
   136→        ),
   137→    }
   138→
   139→    min_delay, max_delay = delay_ranges.get(delay_type, (0.5, 2.0))
   140→
   141→    # Use log-normal instead of uniform
   142→    base_delay = _lognormal_delay(min_delay, max_delay)
   143→
   144→    # Apply fatigue (typing is exempt — fatigue doesn't affect keystroke speed much)
   145→    if delay_type != 'typing':
   146→        base_delay *= _fatigue_multiplier()
   147→        base_delay *= _time_of_day_multiplier()
   148→
   149→    # Add possible distraction pause for non-typing delays
   150→    if delay_type in ('action', 'page_load', 'message'):
   151→        base_delay += _maybe_distraction_pause()
   152→
   153→    return base_delay
   154→
   155→
   156→async def async_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action'):
   157→    """
   158→    Asynchronous version of get_random_delay.
   159→
   160→    Args:
   161→        delay_type: Type of delay - 'page_load', 'action', 'typing', 'scroll', 'message'
   162→    """
   163→    delay = get_random_delay(delay_type)
   164→    await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   165→
   166→
   167→
   168→def get_retry_delay(attempt: int, base_delay: float = 1.0, max_delay: float = 60.0) -> float:
   169→    """
   170→    Calculate exponential backoff delay for retries.
   171→
   172→    Args:
   173→        attempt: Current retry attempt number (0-indexed)
   174→        base_delay: Base delay in seconds
   175→        max_delay: Maximum delay cap
   176→
   177→    Returns:
   178→        Delay in seconds with jitter
   179→    """
   180→    # Exponential backoff: base * 2^attempt
   181→    delay = min(base_delay * (2 ** attempt), max_delay)
   182→
   183→    # Add jitter (±25%)
   184→    jitter = delay * random.uniform(-0.25, 0.25)
   185→    return max(0, delay + jitter)
   186→

1→"""Retry utilities for navigation and network operations."""
     2→
     3→from tenacity import retry, stop_after_attempt, wait_exponential, retry_if_exception_type
     4→from patchright.async_api import Page, Error as PlaywrightError
     5→from loguru import logger
     6→
     7→
     8→@retry(
     9→    stop=stop_after_attempt(3),
    10→    wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=2, max=10),
    11→    retry=retry_if_exception_type((PlaywrightError, TimeoutError, ConnectionError)),
    12→    before_sleep=lambda retry_state: logger.warning(
    13→        f"Navigation failed (attempt {retry_state.attempt_number}), retrying..."
    14→    ),
    15→    reraise=True,
    16→)
    17→async def retry_navigation(page: Page, url: str, **kwargs) -> None:
    18→    """Navigate to a URL with automatic retry on failure.
    19→
    20→    Args:
    21→        page: Playwright page instance
    22→        url: URL to navigate to
    23→        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to page.goto()
    24→    """
    25→    await page.goto(url, **kwargs)
    26→

1→"""Pipeline checkpoint manager for resume capability.
     2→
     3→Saves pipeline progress to a JSON file so that interrupted pipelines
     4→can be resumed from where they left off.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import json
     8→import os
     9→import tempfile
    10→from datetime import datetime
    11→from pathlib import Path
    12→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set
    13→
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→
    17→CHECKPOINT_DIR = Path("data/.checkpoints")
    18→
    19→
    20→class CheckpointManager:
    21→    """Manages pipeline checkpoint save/load for resume support."""
    22→
    23→    def __init__(self, checkpoint_id: Optional[str] = None):
    24→        CHECKPOINT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    25→
    26→        if checkpoint_id:
    27→            self.checkpoint_path = CHECKPOINT_DIR / f"{checkpoint_id}.json"
    28→        else:
    29→            # Generate ID from timestamp
    30→            ts = datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    31→            self.checkpoint_path = CHECKPOINT_DIR / f"pipeline_{ts}.json"
    32→
    33→        self._data: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    34→
    35→    def save(
    36→        self,
    37→        keywords: tuple,
    38→        template: str,
    39→        variables: dict,
    40→        company: tuple,
    41→        location: Optional[str],
    42→        max_pages: int,
    43→        batch_pages: int,
    44→        skip_messaged: bool,
    45→        interactive: bool,
    46→        completed_pages: int,
    47→        sent_ids: List[str],
    48→        seen_ids: List[str],
    49→        total_results: Optional[dict] = None,
    50→        mode: str = "search_center",
    51→        dry_run: bool = False,
    52→    ) -> None:
    53→        """Save current pipeline state to checkpoint file."""
    54→        self._data = {
    55→            "created_at": self._data.get("created_at", datetime.utcnow().isoformat()),
    56→            "updated_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
    57→            "params": {
    58→                "keywords": list(keywords),
    59→                "template": template,
    60→                "variables": variables,
    61→                "company": list(company) if company else [],
    62→                "location": location,
    63→                "max_pages": max_pages,
    64→                "batch_pages": batch_pages,
    65→                "skip_messaged": skip_messaged,
    66→                "interactive": interactive,
    67→                "mode": mode,
    68→                "dry_run": dry_run,
    69→            },
    70→            "progress": {
    71→                "completed_pages": completed_pages,
    72→                "sent_ids": sent_ids,
    73→                "seen_ids": seen_ids,
    74→                "total_results": total_results or {},
    75→            },
    76→        }
    77→
    78→        try:
    79→            tmp_fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(
    80→                dir=self.checkpoint_path.parent, suffix=".tmp"
    81→            )
    82→            try:
    83→                with os.fdopen(tmp_fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    84→                    json.dump(self._data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    85→                os.replace(tmp_path, self.checkpoint_path)
    86→            except BaseException:
    87→                try:
    88→                    os.unlink(tmp_path)
    89→                except OSError:
    90→                    pass
    91→                raise
    92→            logger.debug(f"Checkpoint saved: {self.checkpoint_path}")
    93→        except Exception as e:
    94→            logger.error(f"Failed to save checkpoint: {e}")
    95→            raise
    96→
    97→    @classmethod
    98→    def load_latest(cls) -> Optional["CheckpointManager"]:
    99→        """Load the most recent checkpoint file.
   100→
   101→        Returns:
   102→            CheckpointManager with loaded data, or None if no checkpoint exists.
   103→        """
   104→        CHECKPOINT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   105→        files = sorted(CHECKPOINT_DIR.glob("pipeline_*.json"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
   106→
   107→        if not files:
   108→            return None
   109→
   110→        mgr = cls(checkpoint_id="__loading__")
   111→        mgr.checkpoint_path = files[0]
   112→
   113→        try:
   114→            with open(files[0], "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   115→                mgr._data = json.load(f)
   116→            logger.info(f"Loaded checkpoint: {files[0]}")
   117→            return mgr
   118→        except Exception as e:
   119→            logger.error(f"Failed to load checkpoint: {e}")
   120→            return None
   121→
   122→    @property
   123→    def params(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   124→        return self._data.get("params", {})
   125→
   126→    @property
   127→    def progress(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   128→        return self._data.get("progress", {})
   129→
   130→    @property
   131→    def completed_pages(self) -> int:
   132→        return self.progress.get("completed_pages", 0)
   133→
   134→    @property
   135→    def sent_ids(self) -> List[str]:
   136→        return list(self.progress.get("sent_ids", []))
   137→
   138→    @property
   139→    def seen_ids(self) -> Set[str]:
   140→        return set(self.progress.get("seen_ids", []))
   141→
   142→    @property
   143→    def total_results(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
   144→        return self.progress.get("total_results", {})
   145→
   146→    @property
   147→    def summary(self) -> str:
   148→        """Human-readable summary of checkpoint state."""
   149→        p = self.params
   150→        pr = self.progress
   151→        created = self._data.get("created_at", "unknown")
   152→        updated = self._data.get("updated_at", "unknown")
   153→        mode = p.get("mode", "search_center")
   154→        return (
   155→            f"Keywords: {p.get('keywords', [])}\n"
   156→            f"Template: {p.get('template', '')}\n"
   157→            f"Mode: {mode}\n"
   158→            f"Pages completed: {pr.get('completed_pages', 0)} / {p.get('max_pages', '?')}\n"
   159→            f"Candidates seen: {len(pr.get('seen_ids', []))}\n"
   160→            f"Messages sent: {len(pr.get('sent_ids', []))}\n"
   161→            f"Created: {created}\n"
   162→            f"Last updated: {updated}"
   163→        )
   164→
   165→    def delete(self) -> None:
   166→        """Remove checkpoint file after successful completion."""
   167→        try:
   168→            if self.checkpoint_path.exists():
   169→                self.checkpoint_path.unlink()
   170→                logger.debug(f"Checkpoint deleted: {self.checkpoint_path}")
   171→        except Exception as e:
   172→            logger.error(f"Failed to delete checkpoint: {e}")
   173→

1→"""Candidate scoring for priority-based messaging.
     2→
     3→Scores candidates based on title seniority, company tier, and keyword
     4→relevance so that the best-fit candidates get contacted first.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import re
     8→from typing import Dict, List, Optional
     9→
    10→from loguru import logger
    11→
    12→
    13→# Seniority keywords and their score weights
    14→SENIORITY_SCORES: Dict[str, int] = {
    15→    # C-level
    16→    "CTO": 50, "CEO": 40, "COO": 35, "VP": 40,
    17→    "首席": 45, "总裁": 40, "副总裁": 40,
    18→    # Director
    19→    "总监": 35, "技术总监": 40,
    20→    # Senior management
    21→    "技术负责人": 35, "负责人": 30, "Tech Lead": 35,
    22→    # Senior individual contributors
    23→    "架构师": 35, "专家": 30, "staff": 30, "Staff": 30,
    24→    "principal": 35, "Principal": 35,
    25→    "资深": 25, "高级": 20, "Senior": 20, "senior": 20,
    26→    # Mid-level
    27→    "经理": 15, "主管": 15, "lead": 15, "Lead": 15,
    28→    "中级": 10,
    29→    # Junior
    30→    "初级": 5, "Junior": 5, "junior": 5,
    31→    "助理": 3, "实习": 1,
    32→}
    33→
    34→# Well-known tech companies (tier 1)
    35→TIER1_COMPANIES = {
    36→    "字节跳动", "ByteDance", "腾讯", "Tencent", "阿里巴巴", "阿里", "Alibaba",
    37→    "百度", "Baidu", "美团", "Meituan", "京东", "JD", "拼多多", "PDD",
    38→    "华为", "Huawei", "小米", "Xiaomi", "网易", "NetEase", "快手", "Kwai",
    39→    "蚂蚁集团", "蚂蚁金服", "滴滴", "DiDi",
    40→    "Google", "谷歌", "Microsoft", "微软", "Apple", "苹果",
    41→    "Amazon", "亚马逊", "Meta", "Facebook",
    42→    "Netflix", "Uber", "OpenAI", "Anthropic",
    43→}
    44→
    45→# Good tech companies (tier 2)
    46→TIER2_COMPANIES = {
    47→    "携程", "Trip.com", "B站", "bilibili", "哔哩哔哩",
    48→    "知乎", "Zhihu", "小红书", "得物", "SHEIN",
    49→    "商汤", "旷视", "依图", "寒武纪", "地平线",
    50→    "理想汽车", "蔚来", "小鹏", "大疆", "DJI",
    51→    "米哈游", "miHoYo", "莉莉丝", "叠纸",
    52→    "360", "新浪", "微博", "搜狐", "爱奇艺",
    53→    "OPPO", "vivo", "一加", "联想", "Lenovo",
    54→    "SAP", "Oracle", "IBM", "Adobe", "Salesforce",
    55→    "Stripe", "Airbnb", "Spotify", "Twitter", "X",
    56→}
    57→
    58→
    59→def score_candidate(
    60→    name: str,
    61→    title: str,
    62→    company: str,
    63→    search_keywords: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    64→) -> int:
    65→    """
    66→    Score a candidate based on title, company, and keyword relevance.
    67→
    68→    Higher score = higher priority for messaging.
    69→
    70→    Scoring breakdown:
    71→    - Title seniority: 0-50 points
    72→    - Company tier: 0-30 points
    73→    - Keyword match: 0-20 points
    74→
    75→    Args:
    76→        name: Candidate name
    77→        title: Job title
    78→        company: Company name
    79→        search_keywords: Original search keywords for relevance scoring
    80→
    81→    Returns:
    82→        Integer score (higher = better fit)
    83→    """
    84→    score = 0
    85→
    86→    # 1. Title seniority scoring
    87→    title_lower = title.lower() if title else ""
    88→    best_seniority = 0
    89→    for keyword, points in SENIORITY_SCORES.items():
    90→        if keyword.lower() in title_lower or keyword in title:
    91→            best_seniority = max(best_seniority, points)
    92→    score += best_seniority
    93→
    94→    # 2. Company tier scoring
    95→    # Require minimum 2 characters on both sides to avoid false positives
    96→    # from short names like "X", "JD" matching substrings of unrelated companies
    97→    if company:
    98→        company_lower = company.lower()
    99→        company_matched = False
   100→        for t1 in TIER1_COMPANIES:
   101→            t1_lower = t1.lower()
   102→            if len(t1_lower) >= 2 and len(company_lower) >= 2:
   103→                if t1_lower == company_lower or (len(t1_lower) >= 3 and t1_lower in company_lower) or (len(company_lower) >= 3 and company_lower in t1_lower):
   104→                    score += 30
   105→                    company_matched = True
   106→                    break
   107→        if not company_matched:
   108→            for t2 in TIER2_COMPANIES:
   109→                t2_lower = t2.lower()
   110→                if len(t2_lower) >= 2 and len(company_lower) >= 2:
   111→                    if t2_lower == company_lower or (len(t2_lower) >= 3 and t2_lower in company_lower) or (len(company_lower) >= 3 and company_lower in t2_lower):
   112→                        score += 20
   113→                        break
   114→
   115→    # 3. Keyword relevance (if search keywords provided), capped at 20 points
   116→    if search_keywords and title:
   117→        keyword_score = 0
   118→        for kw in search_keywords:
   119→            kw_lower = kw.lower()
   120→            if kw_lower in title_lower:
   121→                keyword_score += 10
   122→            elif kw_lower in (company or "").lower():
   123→                keyword_score += 5
   124→        score += min(keyword_score, 20)
   125→
   126→    # 4. Bonus: R&D-specific titles
   127→    rd_bonus_patterns = [
   128→        r"工程师", r"开发", r"架构", r"算法", r"研发",
   129→        r"engineer", r"developer", r"architect",
   130→    ]
   131→    for pattern in rd_bonus_patterns:
   132→        if re.search(pattern, title, re.IGNORECASE):
   133→            score += 5
   134→            break
   135→
   136→    return score
   137→
   138→
   139→def sort_candidates_by_score(
   140→    candidates: list,
   141→    search_keywords: Optional[List[str]] = None,
   142→) -> list:
   143→    """
   144→    Sort a list of candidate dicts or objects by score (descending).
   145→
   146→    Works with both CandidateBasic dataclass instances and dicts.
   147→
   148→    Args:
   149→        candidates: List of candidates (dicts or objects with name/title/company)
   150→        search_keywords: Original search keywords for relevance scoring
   151→
   152→    Returns:
   153→        Sorted list (highest score first), each item gets a '_score' attribute/key
   154→    """
   155→    scored = []
   156→    for c in candidates:
   157→        if isinstance(c, dict):
   158→            name = c.get("name", "")
   159→            title = c.get("title", "")
   160→            company = c.get("company", "")
   161→        else:
   162→            name = getattr(c, "name", "")
   163→            title = getattr(c, "title", "")
   164→            company = getattr(c, "company", "")
   165→
   166→        s = score_candidate(name, title, company, search_keywords)
   167→
   168→        if isinstance(c, dict):
   169→            c["_score"] = s
   170→        else:
   171→            c._score = s
   172→
   173→        scored.append((s, c))
   174→
   175→    scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
   176→
   177→    result = [item[1] for item in scored]
   178→
   179→    if result:
   180→        top = scored[0][0]
   181→        bottom = scored[-1][0]
   182→        logger.info(f"Candidates scored: top={top}, bottom={bottom}, count={len(result)}")
   183→
   184→    return result
   185→
